sentences.sentence: Collecting must be one of the most varied of human activities, and it's one that many of us psychologists find fascinating.
sentences.translation: 收藏或许是人类最为多样的活动之一，也是我们许多心理学家觉得十分着迷的一种活动。

structure.text: one of the most varied of human activities
that many of us psychologists find fascinating

difficult_points: it's one 中的 one 指代前面的活动，即收藏是众多活动之一。
that 引导定语从句修饰 one，在从句中作 find...fascinating 的宾语，被提前到句首。
many of us psychologists 意为“我们心理学家中的许多人”，us 与 psychologists 同位。

expressions.text: one of the most varied of

sentences.sentence: Many forms of collecting have been dignified with a technical name: an arctophilist collects teddy bears, a philatelist collects postage stamps, and a deltiologist collects postcards.
sentences.translation: 许多种收藏活动都被冠以了专门的名称：热衷收藏泰迪熊的人叫 arctophilist，收藏邮票的人叫 philatelist，收藏明信片的人叫 deltiologist。

structure.text: have been dignified with a technical name

difficult_points: 冒号引出对 technical name 具体所指的三个例子。
be dignified with 在这里指“被赋予（郑重其事的）名称”，带轻微戏谑意味。
三个并列小句分别用不同的术语指代不同种类的收藏者。

expressions.meaning_zh: 被冠以（郑重其事的）名称

sentences.sentence: Amassing hundreds or even thousands of postcards, chocolate wrappers or whatever, takes time, energy and money that could surely be put to much more productive use.
sentences.translation: 积攒成百上千张明信片、糖纸或其他类似物品，需要耗费时间、精力和金钱，而这些原本完全可以用在更有意义的地方。

structure.text: Amassing hundreds or even thousands of postcards, chocolate wrappers or whatever
that could surely be put to much more productive use

difficult_points: 动名词短语 Amassing... 作主语，指“积攒大量……”这一行为。
or whatever 是口语化说法，表示“或诸如此类的东西”。
that 引导定语从句修饰 time, energy and money，could be put to use 是被动语态，表示“本可以被用于”。

expressions.meaning_zh: 将……用于（更有意义的）用途

sentences.sentence: And yet there are millions of collectors around the world.
sentences.translation: 然而，全世界仍有数百万收藏者。
difficult_points: And yet 承接前文（收藏耗费大量时间精力金钱），表示“尽管如此”的转折。
there are 是存在句结构，真正强调的信息中心是 millions of collectors。

sentences.sentence: Why do they do it?
sentences.translation: 他们为什么要这样做呢？
difficult_points: do 是疑问句中的助动词，用于构成一般疑问句的倒装语序。
it 指代前文所说的收藏行为，即积攒各种物品的做法。

sentences.sentence: There are the people who collect because they want to make money - this could be called an instrumental reason for collecting; that is, collecting as a means to an end.
sentences.translation: 有些人收藏是因为想赚钱——这可以称为一种工具性的收藏动机，也就是说，把收藏当作达到某种目的的手段。

structure.text: because they want to make money
this could be called an instrumental reason for collecting
that is, collecting as a means to an end

difficult_points: who 引导定语从句修饰 the people，说明这类人的特征。
because 从句嵌套在定语从句内部，说明这些人收藏的原因。
this 指代前面“因为想赚钱而收藏”这一现象，破折号后是对其的概括命名。
that is 引出同位语，进一步解释 instrumental reason 的含义，即“工具性/手段性的原因”。

expressions.text: as a means to an end

sentences.sentence: They'll look for, say, antiques that they can buy cheaply and expect to be able to sell at a profit.
sentences.translation: 他们会寻找，比如说，那些能低价买入、并期望能高价卖出获利的古董。

structure.text: and expect to be able to sell at a profit

difficult_points: say 在此是插入语，表示“比如说”，不是动词原形祈使。
that 引导定语从句修饰 antiques，从句主语 they 后接两个并列谓语 can buy 和 expect。
expect to be able to sell 表示“期望能够卖出”，情态含义叠加不定式结构。

sentences.sentence: But there may well be a psychological element, too - buying cheap and selling dear can give the collector a sense of triumph.
sentences.translation: 但这背后也很可能存在心理因素——低价买入、高价卖出能给收藏者带来一种成就感。

structure.text: there may well be a psychological element, too
buying cheap and selling dear can give the collector a sense of triumph
can give the collector a sense of triumph

difficult_points: may well be 表示“很可能存在”，是带推测语气的说法。
破折号后的内容对前一分句进行补充说明，两者之间隐含解释关系。
buying cheap and selling dear 是并列的动名词短语作主语，字面意为“低价买入，高价卖出”。

sentences.sentence: And as selling online is so easy, more and more people are joining in.
sentences.translation: 而且由于网上出售如此便捷，越来越多的人也加入了进来。

structure.text: as selling online is so easy
more and more people are joining in

difficult_points: as 在此引导原因状语从句，意为“由于”。
selling online 是动名词短语作从句主语，指“在线出售（收藏品）”。
join in 表示“加入，参与进来”，in 是副词而非介词。

sentences.sentence: Many collectors collect to develop their social life, attending meetings of a group of collectors and exchanging information on items.
sentences.translation: 许多收藏者收藏是为了丰富自己的社交生活，他们参加收藏者聚会，交流有关藏品的信息。

structure.text: attending meetings of a group of collectors and exchanging information on items

difficult_points: to develop their social life 是不定式作目的状语，说明收藏的原因。
attending... and exchanging... 是现在分词短语，说明收藏过程中伴随的具体行为。
items 泛指他们所收藏的各种物品。

sentences.sentence: This is a variant on joining a bridge club or a gym, and similarly brings them into contact with like-minded people.
sentences.translation: 这与加入桥牌俱乐部或健身房类似，同样能让他们结识志趣相投的人。

structure.text: is a variant on joining a bridge club or a gym
joining a bridge club or a gym
and similarly brings them into contact with like-minded people

difficult_points: This 指代前一句提到的“参加聚会、交流信息”这种收藏方式。
a variant on 表示“……的一种变体/类似形式”。
bring... into contact with 表示“使……与……产生联系”。

sentences.sentence: Another motive for collecting is the desire to find something special, or a particular example of the collected item, such as a rare early recording by a particular singer.
sentences.translation: 另一种收藏动机是渴望找到某件特别的东西，或者是所收藏物品中的某个特定版本，比如某位歌手早期发行的稀有唱片。

structure.text: the desire to find something special, or a particular example of the collected item
to find something special, or a particular example of the collected item
such as a rare early recording by a particular singer

difficult_points: to find... 是不定式短语，修饰 desire，说明这种渴望的具体内容。
something special 和 a particular example of the collected item 是 to find 的并列宾语，指两种可能的目标。
such as 引出具体例子，说明“某位歌手早期的稀有唱片”这类物品。

sentences.sentence: Some may spend their whole lives in a hunt for this.
sentences.translation: 有些人可能会耗费一生去追寻这样的东西。
difficult_points: Some 指代“一些收藏者”，承接上文。
this 指代上一句提到的想要找到的特别物品。

sentences.sentence: Psychologically, this can give a purpose to a life that otherwise feels aimless.
sentences.translation: 从心理层面看，这能为原本可能漫无目的的生活赋予一个目标。

structure.text: can give a purpose to a life

difficult_points: Psychologically 修饰全句，表示“从心理层面而言”。
this 指代前一句“耗费一生寻找某物”这一行为。
otherwise 意为“否则、不然的话”，暗示若无这种追寻，生活会显得漫无目的。

sentences.sentence: There is a danger, though, that if the individual is ever lucky enough to find what they're looking for, rather than celebrating their success, they may feel empty, now that the goal that drove them on has gone.
sentences.translation: 不过，这里也存在一种危险：如果这个人真的幸运到找到了自己苦苦寻觅的东西，他们可能不会为这一成功感到庆幸，反而会感到空虚，因为那个曾经驱使他们不断前行的目标已经不复存在。

structure.text: that if the individual is ever lucky enough to find what they're looking for, rather than celebrating their success, they may feel empty, now that the goal that drove them on has gone
if the individual is ever lucky enough to find what they're looking for
now that the goal that drove them on has gone

difficult_points: that 引导同位语从句，具体说明 danger 的内容是什么。
if 引导条件状语从句，lucky enough to 表示“幸运到足以……”。
rather than celebrating 表示“不是庆祝……，而是……”，与后面 they may feel empty 形成对比。
now that 引导原因状语从句，that drove them on 是定语从句修饰 the goal。

sentences.sentence: If you think about collecting postage stamps, another potential reason for it - or, perhaps, a result of collecting - is its educational value.
sentences.translation: 如果说到集邮，另一个可能的原因——或者说也可能是收藏带来的一个结果——就是它的教育价值。

structure.text: If you think about collecting postage stamps
or, perhaps, a result of collecting

difficult_points: If 引导条件状语从句，起引出话题的作用，相当于“说到……”。
it 指代前面的 collecting postage stamps，即集邮这一行为。
破折号之间的内容是对 reason 的补充说明，表示这也可能是收藏的一种结果而非单纯原因。

sentences.sentence: Stamp collecting opens a window to other countries, and to the plants, animals, or famous people shown on their stamps.
sentences.translation: 集邮为人们打开了一扇了解其他国家的窗口，也让人们认识邮票上所描绘的植物、动物或名人。

structure.text: opens a window to other countries
and to the plants, animals, or famous people

difficult_points: opens a window to 是比喻说法，意为“打开一扇了解……的窗口”。
and to... 与前面的 to other countries 并列，共享谓语 opens a window。
shown on their stamps 是过去分词短语，修饰 plants, animals, or famous people，说明它们是邮票上所描绘的内容。

sentences.sentence: Similarly, in the 19th century, many collectors amassed fossils, animals and plants from around the globe, and their collections provided a vast amount of information about the natural world.
sentences.translation: 同样地，在19世纪，许多收藏者从世界各地收集了大量化石、动物和植物标本，这些收藏为人们提供了关于自然界的大量信息。

structure.text: amassed fossils, animals and plants from around the globe
and their collections provided a vast amount of information about the natural world

difficult_points: Similarly 承接上文，表示与集邮的教育价值类似的情况。
from around the globe 修饰 fossils, animals and plants，说明这些标本来自世界各地。
第二个分句以 their collections 为主语，与前一分句因果衔接：正是这些收藏提供了大量自然知识。

sentences.sentence: Without those collections, our understanding would be greatly inferior to what it is.
sentences.translation: 如果没有这些收藏，我们对自然界的认识将远不如现在这样丰富。

structure.text: our understanding would be greatly inferior to what it is

difficult_points: Without those collections 相当于虚拟条件句 if it were not for those collections，与 would be 构成虚拟语气。
inferior to what it is 表示“比现在的（认知水平）更逊色”，what it is 指代现在实际达到的理解程度。

sentences.sentence: In the past - and nowadays, too, though to a lesser extent - a popular form of collecting, particularly among boys and men, was trainspotting.
sentences.translation: 在过去——如今也是如此，只是程度有所减轻——一种在男孩和男人中尤为流行的收藏方式是记录火车头型号。

structure.text: and nowadays, too, though to a lesser extent

difficult_points: 破折号之间的内容是插入成分，补充说明这种收藏方式在当下依然存在，只是程度较轻。
though to a lesser extent 是省略结构，完整意思是 though it happens to a lesser extent。
particularly among boys and men 修饰 a popular form of collecting，说明这种爱好的主要人群。

sentences.sentence: This might involve trying to see every locomotive of a particular type, using published data that identifies each one, and ticking off each engine as it is seen.
sentences.translation: 这可能包括试图看到某一特定型号的每一台机车，利用能识别每台机车的公开资料，并在见到每台机车后逐一打勾核对。

structure.text: trying to see every locomotive of a particular type
using published data that identifies each one
and ticking off each engine as it is seen

difficult_points: involve 后接三个并列的动名词短语 trying...、using...、ticking off...，都是 involve 的宾语。
that identifies each one 是定语从句修饰 published data，说明这些资料的作用。
as it is seen 中 it 指代 each engine，as 表示“当……的时候”。

sentences.sentence: Train spotters exchange information, these days often by mobile phone, so they can work out where to go to, to see a particular engine.
sentences.translation: 火车迷们会交流信息——如今常常通过手机——这样他们就能弄清楚该去哪里，才能看到某一特定的机车。

structure.text: these days often by mobile phone
so they can work out where to go to

difficult_points: these days often by mobile phone 是插入的方式状语，说明如今常用的交流方式。
so 引导结果状语从句，说明交流信息带来的结果。
where to go to 是疑问词加不定式构成的宾语从句，作 work out 的宾语；末尾 to see a particular engine 是目的状语。

sentences.sentence: As a by-product, many practitioners of the hobby become very knowledgeable about railway operations, or the technical specifications of different engine types.
sentences.translation: 作为一种附带的收获，许多从事这项爱好的人对铁路运营，或是不同机车型号的技术规格都变得非常了解。

structure.text: become very knowledgeable about railway operations, or the technical specifications of different engine types

difficult_points: As a by-product 表示“作为一种附带结果”，说明后面内容并非刻意追求的目标。
practitioners of the hobby 指“从事这项爱好的人”，即前文的 train spotters。
railway operations 与 the technical specifications... 是并列宾语，由 or 连接。

sentences.sentence: Similarly, people who collect dolls may go beyond simply enlarging their collection, and develop an interest in the way that dolls are made, or the materials that are used.
sentences.translation: 同样地，收藏玩偶的人可能不仅仅满足于扩充自己的收藏，还会对玩偶的制作方式，或所用的材料产生兴趣。

structure.text: may go beyond simply enlarging their collection
and develop an interest in the way that dolls are made, or the materials that are used

difficult_points: who collect dolls 是定语从句，修饰 people，说明这些人的身份。
go beyond doing 表示“不仅仅停留在……，而是超越……”。
that dolls are made 和 that are used 分别是定语从句，修饰 the way 和 the materials。

sentences.sentence: These have changed over the centuries from the wood that was standard in 16th century Europe, through the wax and porcelain of later centuries, to the plastics of today's dolls.
sentences.translation: 这些材料历经数个世纪不断变化：从16世纪欧洲通用的木材，经过后来几个世纪的蜡和瓷，一直到如今玩偶所用的塑料。

structure.text: from the wood that was standard in 16th century Europe
that was standard in 16th century Europe
through the wax and porcelain of later centuries
to the plastics of today's dolls

difficult_points: These 指代前一句提到的 the materials that are used，即制作玩偶所用的材料。
from ... through ... to ... 三者并列，勾勒材料演变的时间顺序。
that was standard in 16th century Europe 是定语从句，修饰 wood，说明其在16世纪欧洲的通用地位。

sentences.sentence: Or collectors might be inspired to study how dolls reflect notions of what children like, or ought to like.
sentences.translation: 又或者，收藏者可能会受到启发，去研究玩偶如何反映出人们对于孩子喜欢什么、或者应该喜欢什么的观念。

structure.text: how dolls reflect notions of what children like, or ought to like
what children like, or ought to like

difficult_points: be inspired to do 表示“受到启发去做某事”。
how dolls reflect... 是 study 的宾语从句，探讨玩偶如何体现某些观念。
what children like, or ought to like 是嵌套的宾语从句，or ought to like 与 like 并列，暗含“实际喜欢的”与“应该喜欢的”之间的差异。

sentences.sentence: Not all collectors are interested in learning from their hobby, though, so what we might call a psychological reason for collecting is the need for a sense of control, perhaps as a way of dealing with insecurity.
sentences.translation: 并非所有收藏者都对从爱好中学习感兴趣，因此，我们可以称之为一种心理动机的东西，就是对掌控感的需求，这或许是应对不安全感的一种方式。

structure.text: Not all collectors are interested in learning from their hobby
so what we might call a psychological reason for collecting is the need for a sense of control
what we might call a psychological reason for collecting
perhaps as a way of dealing with insecurity

difficult_points: Not all 是部分否定，意为“并非所有收藏者都……”，而不是“所有收藏者都不……”。
though 在句中作插入语，意为“不过、然而”。
what we might call... 是名词性从句作主语，字面为“我们可以称之为……的东西”，即“所谓的……”。

expressions.meaning_zh: 我们可以称之为的……；所谓的……

sentences.sentence: Stamp collectors, for instance, arrange their stamps in albums, usually very neatly, organising their collection according to certain commonplace principles - perhaps by country in alphabetical order, or grouping stamps by what they depict - people, birds, maps, and so on.
sentences.translation: 例如，集邮者会把邮票整理进集邮册，通常十分整齐，并按照某些常见的原则来编排自己的收藏——可能是按国家名称的字母顺序排列，也可能按邮票所描绘的内容分类，比如人物、鸟类、地图等等。

structure.text: organising their collection according to certain commonplace principles
perhaps by country in alphabetical order, or grouping stamps by what they depict
people, birds, maps, and so on

difficult_points: for instance 是插入语，举例说明前一句提到的“控制感需求”。
organising... 是现在分词短语，说明 arrange 这一动作的具体方式。
破折号之间的内容进一步举例说明 certain commonplace principles 具体指哪些排列原则。
by what they depict 中 what 引导宾语从句，depict 的宾语是 what，指邮票描绘的内容。

sentences.sentence: One reason, conscious or not, for what someone chooses to collect is to show the collector's individualism.
sentences.translation: 一个人选择收藏什么，其中一个原因——无论是否有意为之——是为了展现收藏者的个性。

structure.text: for what someone chooses to collect

difficult_points: conscious or not 是插入的形容词短语，修饰 reason，说明这一原因可能是有意识或无意识的。
what someone chooses to collect 是 for 的宾语从句，指“某人选择收藏什么”。
to show... 是不定式作表语，说明这一原因的具体内容。

sentences.sentence: Someone who decides to collect something as unexpected as dog collars, for instance, may be conveying their belief that they must be interesting themselves.
sentences.translation: 比如，有人决定收藏像狗项圈这样出人意料的东西，这或许是在传达一种信念：他们自己一定是个有趣的人。

structure.text: who decides to collect something as unexpected as dog collars
that they must be interesting themselves

difficult_points: who decides to collect... 是定语从句，修饰 someone，as unexpected as 表示“和……一样出人意料”。
for instance 是插入语，举例说明。
that 引导同位语从句，说明 belief 的具体内容；themselves 起强调作用，强调是“他们自己”有趣。

sentences.sentence: And believe it or not, there is at least one dog collar museum in existence, and it grew out of a personal collection.
sentences.translation: 而且信不信由你，世上至少存在一家狗项圈博物馆，它正是由一个人的私人收藏发展而来的。

structure.text: there is at least one dog collar museum in existence
and it grew out of a personal collection

difficult_points: believe it or not 是固定插入语，表示“信不信由你”。
in existence 修饰 dog collar museum，强调其“确实存在”。
grow out of 表示“由……发展而来”，it 指代前面的博物馆。

sentences.sentence: Of course, all hobbies give pleasure, but the common factor in collecting is usually passion: pleasure is putting it far too mildly.
sentences.translation: 当然，所有爱好都能带来快乐，但收藏中共同的因素往往是热情：用“快乐”来形容，未免太轻描淡写了。

structure.text: but the common factor in collecting is usually passion
pleasure is putting it far too mildly

difficult_points: Of course 表示让步，先承认“所有爱好都带来快乐”这一共识，再用 but 转折。
冒号后的内容对前面 passion 的程度做进一步说明。
pleasure is putting it far too mildly 字面是“用‘快乐’来形容太轻描淡写了”，即这个词远不足以描述这种感受。

expressions.meaning_zh: 说得含蓄，轻描淡写地说

sentences.sentence: More than most other hobbies, collecting can be totally engrossing, and can give a strong sense of personal fulfilment.
sentences.translation: 与大多数其他爱好相比，收藏能让人全神贯注，并带来强烈的个人成就感。

structure.text: and can give a strong sense of personal fulfilment

difficult_points: More than most other hobbies 是比较状语，置于句首，表示“比大多数其他爱好更……”。
engrossing 表示“极其吸引人、令人全神贯注的”。
can be 和 can give 是并列谓语，共用主语 collecting。

expressions.meaning_zh: 完全令人着迷的，极具吸引力的
一种个人成就感/满足感

sentences.sentence: To non-collectors it may appear an eccentric, if harmless, way of spending time, but potentially, collecting has a lot going for it.
sentences.translation: 在非收藏者看来，收藏或许显得是一种古怪、即便无害的消磨时间的方式，但从潜力上看，收藏其实有很多可取之处。

structure.text: it may appear an eccentric, if harmless, way of spending time
but potentially, collecting has a lot going for it

difficult_points: To non-collectors 表示“在非收藏者看来”，限定后面判断的视角。
if harmless 是省略结构，完整为 even if it is harmless，插入在 eccentric 和 way 之间。
have a lot going for it 是习语，表示“有很多优点/有利条件”。

expressions.text: have a lot going for it
